reasoning: Primary amoebic meningoencephalitis (PAM) is an extremely rare and typically fatal disease caused by the amoeba Naegleria fowleri. Existing vaccine research for this disease is restricted to very early pre-clinical stages (in silico models, mouse models). There are no human clinical trials underway, and developing an approved vaccine requires many years, especially for rare diseases where efficacy trials are structurally very difficult to conduct. Consequently, it is virtually impossible for a PAM vaccine to be developed and approved by late 2025. The only realistic mechanism for the page to say a vaccine has been created on the resolution dates would be an errant Wikipedia edit or uncorrected vandalism that survives moderation. Because this possibility exists but is very small, the probabilities are anchored near the absolute minimum allowed (0.02) for near-term dates, increasing very slightly at the longest horizon to reflect generalized distant uncertainty.
